content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

October 21, 1982


Assemblyman Dave Elder
State Capitol, Room 2175
Sacramento, CA. 95814

RE: FPPC No. GC-82/165

Dear Assemblyman Elder:

   As you requested, we have reviewed your Statements of
Economic Interests (SEI) for possible omissions of financial
interests that, under the Political Reform Act (The Act), are
required to be disclosed in such statements. Attached to the
August 16, 1982 letter to you from Alan Horndon is a list of
financial interests that were not included in your SEI's.
Subsequent to that letter, you provided us with documentation
from businesses from which you received loans, detailing the
nature of those financial transactions. That correspondence
serves to justify why those loans were not disclosed on any
of your SEI's. Therefore, the four loans listed below are
no longer in question as to their reportability for the year(s)
indicated:

   1. Loan from Farmers & Merchants Trust in excess of
      $10,000 on Terraine Avenue property (1979, 1980).

   2. Loan from Harbor Bank in excess of $10,000 on
      Terraine Avenue property (1979, 1980).

   3. Loan from C. Robert Langstiet & Son, Inc. in
      excess of $10,000 on Terraine Avenue property (1980).

   4. Loan from Harbor Bank in excess of $10,000 on Mesa
      Avenue property (1979, 1980, 1981).

   However, pursuant to those provisions of the Act discussed
below, the other financial interests listed in the August 16, 1982
attachment should have been reported on your SEI's.

Elder
October 21, 1987
Page two


Government Code Section 87203 1/ provides that,

"Every person who holds an office specified in
Section 87200 [which includes the Legislature]
shall, each year at a time specified by commission
regulations, file a statement disclosing his
investments, his interests in real property, and
his income during the period since the previous
statement filed under this section or Section 87202.
The statement shall include any investments and
interest in real property held at any time during
the period covered by the statement, whether or not
they are still held at the time of filing.(emphasis added)

An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033
to include

"any leasehold, beneficial or ownership interest or
an option to acquire such an interest in real property
located in the jurisdiction owned directly, indirectly
or beneficially by the public official, or other filer,
or his or her immediate family if the fair market value
of the interest is greater than one thousand dollars
($1,000). . . .

Accordingly, under Section 87203, any such interest in real
property held at any time during the reporting period must be
disclosed on the SEI for that period. Section 87206(e) further
requires the filer to disclose, "[i]n the case of a statement
filed under Section 87203 or 87204, if the investment or interest
in real property was partially or wholly acquired or disposed of
during the period covered by the statement, the date of acquisition or disposal." Section 87206 lists all disclosure information
required for real property interests.

For purposes of disclosing income in an SEI pursuant to the
requirements of Section 87207, income is defined in Section 82030(a)
to mean, "any salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent,
proceeds from any sale, gift, . . . , loan, forgiveness or payment
of indebtedness received by the filer, reimbursement for expenses,
per diem, . . ., and including any community property interest in
income of a spouse." (emphasis added) Loans from commercial
lending institutions on terms available to the public which are
used to purchase the filer's principal residence or which are
for less than ten thousand dollars, need not be disclosed.
Section 82030(b)(8). Thus, loans secured by the principal
residence must be disclosed if the funds are used for something
other than purchasing the residence. Section 87204(5) also
requires the annual interest rate and security given for the loan
be disclosed. Although a loan may have been reconveyed or assumed
by someone else during the reporting period, the loan is still a
financial interest held during the period that must be reported.
See Section 87203, supra.


1/ All statutory references are to the California Government Code
unless otherwise noted.

Elder
October 21, 1982
Page three


     The proceeds from the sale of real property includes the
total sale amount: basis and capital gain. Other required
income disclosure information is listed in Section 87207.

     In accordance with those SEI disclosure provisions dis-
cussed above, we ask that you file amendments to your SEI's
in regard to the following financial interests:

4008 Pacific Avenue Interests

1979          Loan from C. Robert Langslot & Son, Inc.
              between $1,000 and $10,000 on Pacific
              Avenue property. (Reconveyed 1/31/79)

1981          Income in excess of $10,000 from Clement
              Mesavage for sale of Pacific Avenue property.

1981          Interest in Pacific Avenue real property
              valued between $10,000 and $100,000.

1981          Loan from Mercury Savings & Loan in excess
              of $10,000 on Pacific Avenue property.
              (Assumed by Mesavage on 5/29/81).

1981          Loan from City National Bank in excess of
              $10,000 on Pacific Avenue property.

750 Terraine Avenue Interests

1978/1979     Rental income of between $1,000 and $10,000 on
              Terraine Avenue property.

1979          Income in excess of $10,000 from Eric Donald for
              sale of Terraine Avenue property.

1979          Loan from Florence MacMillan in excess of $10,000
              on Terraine Avenue property.

1981          Loan from C. Robert Langslot & Son, Inc. in
              excess of $10,000 on Terraine Avenue property.

Elder
October 21, 1982
Page Four


In order to assist you in understanding the Act's SEI
disclosure requirements, I have included a copy of the newly
revised SEI form with instructions. Thank you for your
cooperation in this matter and do not hesitate to contact
either myself or Alan Herndon if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

Steven R. Crooks
Legal Counsel
Enforcement Division

CC: Assemblyman Dave Elder Statements of Economic Interests File
